channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Spencer Pratt: 5 Facts About the Candidate for Los Angeles Mayor
item.description: The reality TV star has used his status as a victim of the Palisades fire to mount an outsider campaign portraying Los Angeles officials as incapable of solving the city’s problems.
item.dc:creator: Conor Dougherty
item.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Spencer Pratt, a reality TV star, has used his status as a victim of last year’s Palisades fire to mount a mayoral campaign against the incumbent, Karen Bass.

item.title: Justice Dept. Won’t Proceed With Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund, Blanche Says
item.description: The acting attorney general told lawmakers that he would leave in place an order forcing the I.R.S. to drop investigations into President Trump, his family and his businesses.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, made clear to a House committee on Tuesday that the fund proposal would be permanently withdrawn.

item.title: Order Shielding Trump Family From I.R.S. Audits Will Remain, Blanche Says
item.description: The acting attorney general said the administration was preserving a broad order protecting the president and his family from audits of already filed returns, despite dropping a $1.8 billion payout fund.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times

item.title: G.O.P. Works to Jump-Start Immigration Bill After Trump Retreat on Fund
item.description: Republicans hoped the acting attorney general’s vow that the administration was “not moving forward” with a fund for people claiming to be victimized by the government would unlock the votes.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni and Robert Jimison
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Thune, the majority leader, at a news conference on Tuesday.

item.title: CBS News Fires Scott Pelley of ‘60 Minutes’
item.description: Mr. Pelley, a former “CBS Evening News” anchor, was ousted after months of tensions between staff and Bari Weiss, the network’s editor in chief.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:credit: Michele Crowe/CBS, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Scott Pelley was fired a day after a tense meeting where he accused the CBS editor in chief, Bari Weiss, of “murdering ‘60 Minutes.’”

item.title: Supreme Court Clears the Way for Republican-Friendly Map in Alabama
item.description: In the first major case since the justices narrowed the Voting Rights Act, the court cleared the way for Alabama to use a map preferred by Republicans that leaves the state with only one majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The Supreme Court ruled in 2023 that an Alabama congressional district map with only one majority-Black district diluted the power of Black voters.

item.title: Graham Platner Meets With Senate Democrats Amid Texting Scandal
item.description: Mr. Platner, the likely Democratic nominee for Senate in Maine, met in Washington with several senators. Chuck Schumer, the minority leader, dodged questions about his confidence in Mr. Platner.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/Getty Images
item.media:description: Graham Platner met with Democratic senators in Washington days after reports that he had sent sexual messages to women outside his marriage.

item.title: Top Texas Election Official to Resign, Months Before General Contest
item.description: Jane Nelson, the Texas secretary of state, was appointed to the role overseeing statewide elections in 2023 by Gov. Greg Abbott.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Adam Powell/USA Today Network, via Reuters
item.media:description: Jane Nelson, the top election official in Texas, did not give a reason for her departure.

item.title: Why the U.S.-Iran Negotiations Are Taking So Long
item.description: As negotiations to end the war in Iran drag on, our reporter David E. Sanger describes the factors that are complicating any agreement.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Ray Whitehouse, Jon Miller and Stephanie Swart
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Majid Saeedi/Getty Images

item.title: War Games and Warnings on Strait of Hormuz Went Unheeded by Trump
item.description: Over the past two decades, Iran repeatedly threatened to close down the waterway. President Trump underestimated Iran’s ability to do so.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz has become a huge source of leverage in negotiations with President Trump.

item.title: U.S. Is Said to Be Investigating George Santos Over Kalshi Betting
item.description: The former congressman was referred to federal authorities after he bet on his own attendance at the State of the Union address, a person familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: David Yaffe-Bellany and Sharon LaFraniere
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The inquiry into former Representative George Santos is the third insider trading case to come to light in recent weeks.

item.title: Rubio Suggests U.S. Return to Global Vaccine Program in Rebuke of Kennedy
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated in pointed testimony to senators that he was reclaiming control of the U.S. relationship with Gavi, an international vaccine alliance.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: The State Department is “going to be re-engaging on the issue of Gavi,” Secretary of State Marco Rubio said in a hearing on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Endorses Abelardo De La Espriella, Right-Wing Presidential Candidate in Colombia
item.description: Mr. Trump said that the candidate, Abelardo De La Espriella, was important for the U.S.-Colombia relationship and called his left-wing rival a “Radical Left Marxist.”
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Abelardo De La Espriella at his closing rally in Medellín, Colombia. He gave campaign speeches from a bulletproof booth.

item.title: The Toll of a ‘Missing Scientists’ Conspiracy Theory on the Families Left Behind
item.description: The claim that their loved ones’ deaths and disappearances are linked is almost certainly false — but the loss remains real.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Nazaryan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Rebecca Noble for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elizabeth Weiss, an anthropologist whose husband, Nick Pope, a prominent U.F.O. researcher, died in April. “If you start looking for patterns, you will find them,” she said, quoting him.

item.title: A Stray Dog Became a Cultural Symbol in Brazil. Now, Mexico Is Claiming It.
item.description: To Brazilians, the ubiquitous caramel-colored mutts are a national icon. But a Mexican state has adopted them as its own homegrown symbol.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and María Magdalena Arréllaga
item.media:description: Tiana, a “caramelo,” at a park in Rio de Janeiro in April.

item.title: Scientists Find Way to Supercharge Dangerous Computer ‘Worms’ With A.I.
item.description: Researchers at the University of Toronto showed how hackers could use artificial intelligence to create a program that could target any known flaw in the world’s computers.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz
item.media:credit: Brendan George Ko for The New York Times
item.media:description: Nicolas Papernot, a professor at the University of Toronto, led a team that demonstrated a way of building a dangerous computer “worm” using artificial intelligence.

item.title: In Her Memoir, Jill Biden Is a Watchful Spouse Who Didn’t Always Speak Up About Joe Biden
item.description: The former first lady’s new book reflects an insular White House where loyalty was prized and President Joseph R. Biden Jr.’s feelings were prioritized over health concerns.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.media:credit: Erin Schaff/The New York Times
item.media:description: Jill Biden in 2022. In her telling, by the time she got a glimpse of what others had feared about her husband as president, it was too late.

item.title: Book Review: ‘View From the East Wing,’ by Jill Biden
item.description: Beyond a few pointed digs at her husband’s successor, “View From the East Wing” largely sticks to the head-spinning details of first lady-hood.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Jacobs
item.category: View from the East Wing: A Memoir (Book)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Will Oliver
item.media:description: Jill Biden returning to the White House in January 2025, weeks before her husband finished his term as president.

item.title: Peabo Bryson, Soul Singer Known as the Voice of Love, Dies at 75
item.description: He was admired for impassioned duets with singers like Roberta Flack and Celine Dion and for the Disney hits “Beauty and the Beast” and “A Whole New World.”
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.media:credit: Steve Grayson/WireImage, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Peabo Bryson in 2005. His songs, which dealt with complex relationships and passionate love, dominated the soul and R&B charts for nearly two decades.

item.title: Carney Says Canada Faces a ‘Crisis of Antisemitism’
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s comments followed calls for action from Jewish organizations and after a government report found that antisemitism had surged in Canada in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.media:credit: Chris Young/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada speaking at Holy Blossom Temple in Toronto on Monday.

item.title: ‘La La Land’ Orchestral Performance Saved by Keyboardist in the Audience
item.description: After a musician fell ill during a live performance of the score from “La La Land,” the composer Justin Hurwitz asked for a sight reader. A 21-year-old student stepped up.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses

item.title: Trump Has Failed as Commander in Chief
item.description: Trump has failed to unite the country while at war and instead is seeking personal gain.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Naila Ruechel for The New York Times. Source Photographs by Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times and Getty Images.

item.title: I Went to Lebanon and Found Something Strangely Hopeful
item.description: If given the chance, the country could remake itself on its own terms.
item.dc:creator: Lydia Polgreen and William Keo
item.media:credit: William Keo for The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Wants Trump to Give Peace Talks a Chance
item.description: “Oh, I’m sorry, is wrapping up the war you started not interesting enough?” Michael Kosta said on Tuesday, after the president called negotiations with Iran “boring.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
